title: Arenas, Pools, Slabs, and Fragmentation
description: A game simulation creates millions of short-lived entities and must trade individual release for bounded phase ownership. Learners cumulatively produce instrumented arena and object pool through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Implement checked alignment and bump arithmetic with capacity, high-water, reset, and out-of-memory evidence.
Bind every arena allocation to a phase or owner whose reset cannot race with surviving borrows.
Maintain free-list membership, generation, duplicate-release, and exhaustion invariants.
